search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae rashes children telehealth guideline PDF
site:dha.gov.ae atopic dermatitis telehealth guideline PDF
candidate_sources_rejected: single-diagnosis drug-eruption summaries as broad rash coverage
rejection_reasons: Patient galleries are not clinician guidance.
A single diagnosis cannot cover a broad rash workflow.
Image classifiers are not authoritative clinical sources.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for pediatric broad-rash history and atopic-dermatitis-compatible findings; adult broad rash and many alternative diagnoses are not covered.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth in the stated pediatric or atopic contexts; partial for broad in-person dermatology assessment.
UAE_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai for the stated populations; RCUK anaphylaxis warnings require UAE pathway review.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 rash and atopic documents plus the current official RCUK anaphylaxis page were reviewed on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer DHA issues were identified; the official RCUK page still identifies the May 2021 dedicated guideline as its current version.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to enforce the pediatric population boundary; no content was mapped from scope alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to understand fever and itching groupings without assigning a diagnosis; no content was mapped from grouping alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped pediatric rash history, distribution, morphology, symptom, exposure, medication, impact, and observed skin fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped pediatric fever, pain, immune compromise, uncertainty, worsening, return, and follow-up prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped eczema or dermatitis terminology and compatible itch, scale, vesicle, excoriation, and erythema fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped fever, painful or rapidly worsening eczema, blister, and infection-warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented skin care, trigger, medicine, education, and question fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped breathing and facial or lip swelling warnings only; skin findings alone are explicitly not treated as anaphylaxis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Adult undifferentiated rash, mucosal involvement, drug eruptions, generalized blistering or peeling, sick contacts, wound fields, bloods, swabs, fixed one-week timing, and many diagnosis-specific treatments remain unsupported.
Contact dermatitis or eczema remains clinician-stated and the broad workflow must not infer anaphylaxis from skin findings alone.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
